title: Aluminum - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Trading Economics reports that aluminum fell to 3,561.65 USD/tonne on May 18, 2026, down 0.07% from the previous day. It also notes that aluminum is still up sharply over the past year and provides a near-term outlook above current levels.

title: Aluminium Price Today
summary: Investing.com shows aluminum futures at 3,561.65, unchanged from the previous close. The page also references market commentary about Comex aluminum premiums easing on tariff rollback expectations, suggesting tariff policy remains an active driver of pricing.

title: Major US aluminum producer boosts production to full capacity
summary: A Fox Business segment reports that Century Aluminum is boosting production to full capacity. The piece ties the move to tariff policy and discusses how increased domestic output is affecting jobs and manufacturing capacity.

title: Smelter closure to wipe nearly 30% of US aluminum capacity
summary: Fastmarkets reports that Magnitude 7 Metals planned to curtail operations at its Marston, Missouri smelter because high energy costs made the plant financially unfeasible. The closure would remove a large share of U.S. primary aluminum smelting capacity and leave only four primary smelters operating in the country.

title: New filter captures and recycles aluminum from manufacturing waste
summary: MIT engineers developed a nanofiltration process that can capture aluminum ions from manufacturing waste streams before they are lost in effluent. In lab tests, the membrane selectively captured more than 99 percent of aluminum ions, potentially increasing yield while reducing hazardous waste.

title: Roadmap to net-zero
summary: Hydro outlines its efforts to reduce the carbon footprint of aluminum production through recycling and lower-emission raw materials. The page also notes that technology pioneered at Hydro’s recycling hub in Dormagen, Germany, was introduced to the U.S. market in September 2024.

title: 3 technologies helping the aluminium industry decarbonize
summary: This article highlights several technologies that could help decarbonize aluminum production, including cleaner smelting, improved recycling, and low-emission refining methods. It also discusses industry collaboration and commitments to source more near-zero-emission aluminum by 2030.

title: Decarbonizing Aluminum: Rolling Out a More Sustainable Sector
summary: CSIS examines how recycling, new smelting technologies, and industrial policy are helping reduce emissions in the aluminum sector. The piece also references ELYSIS, a carbon-free smelting approach, and other efforts to lower emissions across production and refining.

title: ARTICLE: Update on the Aluminum Industry Response to Climate Change
summary: This article reviews the aluminum industry's response to climate change, with a focus on low-CO2 aluminum and emerging sustainability trends. It emphasizes the importance of pilot projects to test how smelters can run on renewable power.

title: Steel prices retreat from nine-month highs as seasonal slowdown weighs on demand
summary: Steel rebar futures fell below CNY 3,200 per ton after reaching nine-month highs, as expectations of a seasonal slowdown in demand and softer downstream consumption pressured the market. Mysteel’s latest monthly report also pointed to weakening supply-demand fundamentals that could keep construction steel prices under pressure in China this month.

title: HRC Steel slips from recent highs after strong run
summary: Hot-rolled coil steel prices eased to 1,128.02 USD/T, down from a recent high of 1,143.00 USD/T, though prices remain elevated versus recent periods. The move suggests some cooling after the recent rally in steel prices.

title: HRC rally pushes US Galvalume price up by $30/t in April despite soft demand
summary: Fastmarkets reports that the US domestic Galvalume price increased by $30 per ton in April even though end-demand remained soft. The price move was driven primarily by continued strength in the hot-rolled coil market rather than a broad improvement in downstream demand.

title: Nucor hikes spot HR price to $1,090/ton amid stronger domestic pricing
summary: Steel Market Update reports that Nucor raised its consumer spot price for hot-rolled coil to $1,090 per short ton, up $10 from the prior week. The update comes alongside signs of stronger domestic steel production and a surge in apparent steel supply from February to March.

title: EU steel prices sustained by trade measures: Assofermet
summary: Argus reports that EU mills are keeping steel prices supported mainly through trade barriers rather than firm end-user demand. Italian distributors continued to see weak sales and lower prices, while uncertainty around tariffs and revised safeguard measures weighed on sentiment.

title: Industry Data - American Iron and Steel Institute
summary: AISI’s latest weekly data shows U.S. domestic raw steel production at 1,898,000 net tons for the week ending May 16, 2026, with capability utilization at 82.2%. The prior week ending May 9, 2026, recorded 1,880,000 net tons and 81.4% utilization, indicating a modest week-over-week increase in output and operating rates.

title: United States Steel Production - Trading Economics
summary: Trading Economics reports U.S. steel production increased to 7.2 million thousand tonnes in March 2026 from 6.5 million thousand tonnes in February 2026. The page provides a historical series and highlights that current production remains well below the long-run peak reached in 1973.

title: Copper futures slip to near two-week lows as equities sell off; China demand weighs on prices
summary: Copper futures fell to around $6.2 per pound, their lowest level in nearly two weeks, as a broader selloff in equities pressured commodity markets. The report also notes that elevated copper prices have discouraged buying activity in China, adding to the weakness.

title: Copper Futures Price Today
summary: Copper futures were last quoted at 6.2530, down from a previous close of 6.3340. The page also highlights Reuters coverage that Chilean miner Codelco's El Teniente smelter is set to restart after a collapse, a development that could affect near-term supply conditions.

title: Copper Price (Monthly) - Historical Data & Trends
summary: YCharts shows copper at 12,950.96 USD/mt for April 2026, up 3.37% from the previous month and 41.13% from a year earlier. While not a news article, it provides a recent trend snapshot suggesting strong longer-term gains despite short-term volatility.

title: Nickel futures extend losses as softer ore and sulfate prices weigh on sentiment
summary: Nickel futures traded around $18,500 per tonne, extending losses toward a one-month low. The report says weaker nickel ore prices in the Philippines and easing nickel sulfate prices have reduced near-term support and reinforced expectations of lower production costs across the supply chain.

title: Nickel price update: supply disruptions keep bullish momentum intact
summary: Nickel prices were also reported above $19,500 per tonne, near a two-year high, as supply disruptions and tighter raw material availability continued to support the market. Indonesia's mining quota cuts and a sulfur shortage linked to Strait of Hormuz disruptions added to cost pressures and output constraints.

title: Nickel prices outlook remains bearish as UBS warns of persistent surplus
summary: Investing.com highlights a bearish outlook for nickel, citing UBS expectations that the market will remain in surplus through 2026 despite recent production cuts. The note suggests that supply remains ample enough to cap sustained upside in prices.

title: World's Biggest Nickel Mine in Indonesia Told to Slash Output
summary: Indonesia has reportedly told PT Weda Bay Nickel, the world's biggest nickel mine, to sharply reduce its ore production quota this year. The move is part of the country's effort to support nickel prices, with the quota said to fall from 42 million tons in 2025 to 12 million tons this year, though the company plans to seek a revision.

title: Indonesia navigates nickel market with output cuts, policy shifts
summary: Indonesia is planning to cut nickel output in 2026 as part of a broader policy shift aimed at supporting prices and improving government revenue. The article says the country is adjusting its mining quota approach to manage supply volatility and market confusion.

title: Italy's Cogne Acciai Speciali to acquire majority stake in its scrap supplier
summary: Cogne Acciai Speciali signed a contract to acquire a 65% controlling interest in Com.Steel Inox, its stainless steel scrap supplier. The move is intended to improve the company’s environmental footprint and optimize operations, and the transaction was expected to close in the coming months.

title: Italy's Cogne Acciai Speciali acquires 65 percent of Inox
summary: This report says Cogne Acciai Speciali completed the acquisition of a 65% stake in Com.steel Inox S.p.A. for €24 million. The company said the upstream integration supports its circularity commitments and strengthens its supply chain.

title: Italian steelmaker Cogne Acciai Speciali sourcing all energy from renewables
summary: Cogne Acciai Speciali is now sourcing all of its power needs in Europe from renewable energy. It also plans to add a hydroelectric plant and a 1-MW electrolyzer at its Aosta site to further reduce its carbon footprint and produce green hydrogen.

title: Cogne Acciai Speciali is resetting its carbon footprint with renewable energy
summary: The article says Cogne Acciai Speciali is advancing renewable energy projects to meet all of its energy needs from renewable sources. Its plan includes a hydroelectric power plant and a 1 MW electrolyzer at its main plant in Aosta.

title: Italy's Cogne Acciai Speciali launches green hydrogen pilot plant
summary: Cogne Acciai Speciali has started operations at a pilot green hydrogen plant in Aosta, northern Italy. The project supports the company's broader decarbonization strategy and use of renewable energy in steel production.

title: Prysmian shares rise as Italian cable maker seen as winner amid U.S. copper tariffs
summary: Prysmian shares rose as investors expected the company to benefit from U.S. tariffs on copper. Reuters said Prysmian’s large U.S. business could gain because it sources copper locally and may be able to pass higher costs on to customers.
